73721.policyCitation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.1 — Advanced Imaging of the Knee
73721.policyText: MRI of the knee requires at least 6 weeks of documented conservative treatment (physical therapy, NSAIDs, bracing, or activity modification) and a diagnosis consistent with osteoarthritis, ligamentous injury, or meniscal derangement.

73722.policyCitation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.1 — Advanced Imaging of the Knee
73722.policyText: MRI of the knee with contrast follows the same criteria as 73721.

73723.policyCitation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.1 — Advanced Imaging of the Knee
73723.policyText: MRI of the knee with and without contrast follows the same criteria as 73721.

73221.policyCitation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.3 — Advanced Imaging of the Shoulder
73221.policyText: MRI of the shoulder requires 6 weeks of conservative treatment and a diagnosis of rotator cuff pathology, sprain, or chronic shoulder pain.

72148.policyText: MRI of the lumbar spine requires 6 weeks of conservative treatment and a diagnosis of disc disorder, radiculopathy, or nerve root compression. Office and outpatient hospital settings only.

72149.policyText: MRI of the lumbar spine with contrast follows the same criteria as 72148.

72141.policyText: MRI of the cervical spine requires 4 weeks of conservative treatment and a cervical disc or radiculopathy diagnosis.

70553.policyCitation: BlueCare PPO §4.1.1 — Brain MRI with and without Contrast
70553.policyText: Brain MRI with and without contrast is indicated for suspected intracranial pathology. No conservative-treatment prerequisite when red-flag symptoms are documented.

70551.policyCitation: BlueCare PPO §4.1.2 — Brain MRI without Contrast
70551.policyText: Brain MRI without contrast is indicated for headache workup, stroke evaluation, or suspected lesion.

74177.policyText: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is indicated for acute abdomen, suspected IBD, suspected mass, or biliary pathology.

71260.policyText: CT chest with contrast is indicated for acute chest pain workup, suspected pulmonary embolism, or lung mass evaluation.

29881.policyText: Knee arthroscopy with meniscectomy requires 8 weeks of failed conservative treatment and documented meniscal pathology.

27447.policyText: TKA requires 12 weeks of failed conservative treatment, documented osteoarthritis, and radiographic evidence of joint-space narrowing.

63030.policyText: Lumbar laminectomy/discectomy requires 12 weeks of failed conservative treatment and confirmed disc pathology or radiculopathy.

93458.policyText: Cardiac catheterization with coronary angiography is indicated for suspected coronary artery disease with positive stress test or unstable symptoms.
